title: Garlicky Crispy Vietnamese Lamb Stir-Fry
description: A simple and flavorful Vietnamese-inspired stir-fry with crispy lamb loin chops and a hint of garlic, perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon. This recipe is nut-free friendly and can be ready in under 30 minutes.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb loin chops and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Transfer the lamb to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet or wok and swirl to coat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced onion and bell pepper and cook for 3-4 minutes, until tender-crisp.
Add the fish sauce, soy sauce, and honey to the skillet or wok and stir to combine.
Return the lamb to the skillet or wok and toss to coat with the sauce.
Season with black pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
